title: Indulgent Egyptian Halal-Friendly Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Middle Eastern dish featuring tender lamb, aromatic spices, and a tangy soy sauce glaze. This recipe is adapted to be halal-friendly by using halal-friendly alternatives to conventional ingredients.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Halal-friendly alternative to traditional soy sauce.

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown the lamb shoulder on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Transfer the pot to the oven and cook, covered, at 300°F for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the soy sauce and shio-koji and continue to cook for an additional 10-15 minutes.
Garnish with fresh parsley and lemon juice before serving.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
Let the tagine rest for 10-15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
If desired, serve with rice or couscous to soak up the flavorful sauce.
This tagine can also be made in a slow cooker. Simply brown the lamb and cook the onion mixture in a skillet, then transfer everything to the slow cooker and cook on low for 6-8 hours.
